# Envoy External Processing Filter Migration Guide: v1.30 → v1.31 ## Overview This guide provides comprehensive information for migrating the Envoy External Processing (ext_proc) HTTP filter from version 1.30.0 to version 1.31.0. The upgrade includes: - **New route cache action configuration** providing more granular control over route cache clearing behavior - **Deferred stream closure timeout** for improved observability mode handling - **Enhanced observability mode** with better stream lifecycle management - **HTTP status code change** for timeout errors (504 instead of 500) - **Improved gRPC tracing context propagation** The migration involves both configuration changes and behavioral adjustments. While most existing configurations remain backward compatible, new features are available to improve performance and observability. --- ## Breaking Changes ### 1. Route Cache Clearing Behavior (HIGH PRIORITY) **What Changed:** The route cache clearing behavior has been refactored from a simple boolean flag to a three-state enum, with changes to the clearing logic. **Impact Level:** MEDIUM **Details:** In v1.30, route cache clearing was controlled by a single boolean field: - `disable_clear_route_cache`: If `true`, prevents clearing the route cache In v1.31, this has been replaced by a more flexible enum-based approach: ```protobuf enum RouteCacheAction { DEFAULT = 0; // Clear if clear_route_cache is set AND header mutation exists CLEAR = 1; // Always clear (ignoring the clear_route_cache bit) RETAIN = 2; // Never clear (equivalent to disable_clear_route_cache = true) } ``` **Key Behavioral Differences:** | Scenario | v1.30 Behavior | v1.31 Behavior (route_cache_action) | |----------|----------------|--------------------------------------| | No header mutation | Route cache NOT cleared | Route cache NOT cleared (regardless of `route_cache_action`); `clear_route_cache_ignored` stat incremented | | Header mutation + `clear_route_cache=true` + `disable_clear_route_cache=false` | Cache cleared | **Depends on mode:**
- DEFAULT: Cache cleared
- CLEAR: Cache cleared
- RETAIN: NOT cleared | | Upstream filter chain | Route cache NOT cleared | Route cache NOT cleared (upstream route cache not clearable) | **Configuration Examples:** Before (v1.30): ```yaml ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster disable_clear_route_cache: false # Default behavior ``` After (v1.31) - Equivalent configurations: ```yaml # Option 1: DEFAULT behavior (matches v1.30 behavior) ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: DEFAULT # Option 2: Always clear (when mutations occur) ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: CLEAR # Option 3: Never clear (equivalent to v1.30's disable_clear_route_cache=true) ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: RETAIN ``` **Backward Compatibility Note:** The field `disable_clear_route_cache` is still supported in v1.31 with a migration annotation. When `disable_clear_route_cache=true` is set, it is automatically converted to `route_cache_action=RETAIN`. However, **you cannot set both fields to non-default values** - this will cause a configuration validation error: > "disable_clear_route_cache and route_cache_action can not be set to none-default at the same time." --- ### 2. External Processor Timeout Error Status Code **What Changed:** Timeout errors from the external processor now return HTTP 504 (Gateway Timeout) instead of HTTP 500 (Internal Server Error). **Impact Level:** LOW **Details:** **v1.30 Behavior:** When a message timeout occurred waiting for a response from the external processor: - Return status code: **500 Internal Server Error** - Error response was sent to downstream clients **v1.31 Behavior:** Same timeout condition: - Return status code: **504 Gateway Timeout** - More semantically correct for upstream service timeouts **Why This Matters:** - Downstream clients may differentiate handling based on status code - Monitoring/alerting systems may track 5xx vs 4xx errors differently - Load balancers may handle 504 differently than 500 (some don't retry 504s by default) **Migration Action:** - Review downstream client error handling code - Update monitoring/alerting rules that count 500 errors - Verify load balancer retry policies handle 504 appropriately - Test retry behavior with external processor timeouts --- ## Deprecated Features ### 1. `disable_clear_route_cache` Field (DEPRECATION) **Deprecation Status:** DEPRECATED (still functional in v1.31, planned for removal in v1.32+) **Details:** The boolean field `disable_clear_route_cache` is now superseded by the `route_cache_action` enum: ```protobuf // v1.30 & v1.31 (deprecated in v1.31) bool disable_clear_route_cache = 11; // v1.31 (new, recommended) RouteCacheAction route_cache_action = 18; ``` **Migration Path:** | Current Configuration | Migration Path | |----------------------|----------------| | `disable_clear_route_cache: false` (default) | Use `route_cache_action: DEFAULT` or omit (DEFAULT is the default) | | `disable_clear_route_cache: true` | Use `route_cache_action: RETAIN` | | Not set (defaults to false) | No action needed; system defaults to DEFAULT behavior | **Recommended Timeline:** 1. **Immediately:** Migrate to `route_cache_action` in new configurations 2. **Within 1-2 releases:** Update existing configurations to use `route_cache_action` 3. **Future release:** `disable_clear_route_cache` will be removed --- ## New Features ### 1. Route Cache Action Enum **Availability:** v1.31.0+ **Purpose:** Provides three distinct strategies for handling route cache clearing in response to external processor responses. **Configuration Field:** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { enum RouteCacheAction { DEFAULT = 0; CLEAR = 1; RETAIN = 2; } RouteCacheAction route_cache_action = 18; } ``` **Use Cases:** - **DEFAULT (recommended):** Respects the external processor's `clear_route_cache` flag only when header mutations are present. Best for most use cases where the processor decides when cache clearing is needed. - **CLEAR:** Always clears the route cache when the external processor responds to request headers. Useful when you want to ensure routes are recalculated after every ext_proc interaction, regardless of the processor's response. - **RETAIN:** Never clears the route cache, ignoring any `clear_route_cache` requests from the processor. Use when you're confident routes don't change or want to maximize route cache hit rates. **Configuration Examples:** ```yaml # Example 1: DEFAULT mode (recommended) - name: ext_proc_default typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: DEFAULT processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND response_header_mode: SEND # Example 2: CLEAR mode (always refresh routes) - name: ext_proc_always_clear typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: CLEAR processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND response_header_mode: SEND # Example 3: RETAIN mode (never clear cache) - name: ext_proc_never_clear typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: RETAIN processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND response_header_mode: SEND ``` --- ### 2. Deferred Stream Closure Timeout **Availability:** v1.31.0+ **Purpose:** Controls how long gRPC streams to the external processor remain open after filter destruction in observability mode. **Configuration Field:** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { google.protobuf.Duration deferred_close_timeout = 19; } ``` **Default Value:** 5000 milliseconds (5 seconds) **Details:** In observability mode (`observability_mode: true`), the external processor stream may be kept open independently of the main HTTP request/response stream. This timeout controls how long Envoy will wait before forcefully closing that stream if it hasn't closed gracefully. **When This Matters:** - **Observability mode enabled:** The external processor receives messages asynchronously and may process them after the HTTP response completes - **Filter destruction:** When the filter is destroyed (request complete), instead of immediately closing the stream, it defers the closure - **Timeout:** If the stream doesn't close naturally within `deferred_close_timeout`, Envoy forcefully terminates it **Configuration Examples:** ```yaml # Example 1: Default timeout (5 seconds) ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 5s # Optional, this is the default # Example 2: Shorter timeout (1 second) for faster cleanup ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 1s # Example 3: Longer timeout (10 seconds) to allow more processing time ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 10s ``` **Performance Implications:** - **Too short:** External processor may not complete processing; stream closed prematurely - **Too long:** More memory/connection overhead; slower resource cleanup - **Optimal:** Choose based on typical external processor response time **Related to Observability Mode:** The `deferred_close_timeout` is specifically for the async/deferred handling in observability mode: ```yaml ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true # Enable "Send and Go" mode deferred_close_timeout: 5s # Control cleanup timing processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND request_body_mode: STREAMED response_header_mode: SEND response_body_mode: STREAMED ``` In observability mode: 1. Envoy sends messages to the external processor without waiting for responses 2. The processor observes data but responses are ignored 3. When the HTTP stream ends, the ext_proc stream is marked for deferred closure 4. After `deferred_close_timeout` expires, the stream is forcefully closed --- ### 3. Enhanced Observability Mode **Availability:** v1.31.0+ (improved from v1.30) **Enhancements:** 1. **Better Stream Lifecycle Management:** Streams are now managed by a thread-local stream manager, preventing premature closure while the external processor is still processing 2. **Deferred Closure:** Streams remain open for a configured duration after the HTTP request completes, allowing the external processor to complete its work 3. **Flow Control Support:** Added support for gRPC side-stream flow control with watermark callbacks 4. **Safer Async Processing:** Improved handling of filter destruction timing to prevent use-after-free bugs **Configuration:** ```yaml ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 5s processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND request_body_mode: STREAMED response_header_mode: SEND response_body_mode: STREAMED ``` **Key Points:** - The external processor should **not** send response messages; they are ignored - Only STREAMED and NONE body processing modes are supported - Flow control is now available to prevent overwhelming the processor - Deferred closure ensures graceful stream cleanup --- ## Configuration Updates ### Step 1: Identify Current Usage of `disable_clear_route_cache` Search your Envoy configurations for any use of `disable_clear_route_cache`: ```bash # Find all ext_proc configurations with disable_clear_route_cache grep -r "disable_clear_route_cache" /path/to/configs/ ``` ### Step 2: Update Route Cache Configuration For each configuration using `disable_clear_route_cache`: **If `disable_clear_route_cache: false` or omitted (default):** ```yaml # Old ext_proc: disable_clear_route_cache: false # New (explicit, recommended) ext_proc: route_cache_action: DEFAULT ``` **If `disable_clear_route_cache: true`:** ```yaml # Old ext_proc: disable_clear_route_cache: true # New ext_proc: route_cache_action: RETAIN ``` ### Step 3: Consider Using CLEAR Mode if Applicable If you want to ensure routes are always refreshed after ext_proc processing: ```yaml ext_proc: route_cache_action: CLEAR grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster ``` ### Step 4: Configure Deferred Close Timeout (if using observability_mode) If observability mode is enabled, explicitly set the deferred close timeout based on your workload: ```yaml ext_proc: observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 5s # Adjust based on processor response time grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster ``` ### Step 5: Test Error Handling for 504 Responses Update any downstream error handling code that specifically catches or handles 500 errors from ext_proc timeouts: ```python # Example: If you had code like this try: response = requests.get(envoy_url) except requests.HTTPError as e: if e.response.status_code == 500: # Might have been an ext_proc timeout # Now it will be 504 instead except requests.HTTPError as e: if e.response.status_code == 504: # This is now the ext_proc timeout code ``` --- ## Behavioral Changes ### 1. Route Cache Clearing Logic **v1.30 Logic:** ``` if clear_route_cache AND NOT disable_clear_route_cache: clear_route_cache() else: do_nothing() ``` **v1.31 Logic:** ``` if no header mutation: do_nothing() # No reason to clear else: switch route_cache_action: case DEFAULT: if clear_route_cache: clear_route_cache() case CLEAR: clear_route_cache() # Always case RETAIN: do_nothing() # Never ``` **Key Difference:** v1.31 adds a check for header mutations, preventing unnecessary cache clearing when headers aren't modified. **Impact:** - Fewer unnecessary route cache clears - Better performance in scenarios with many header-only requests - More explicit control through `route_cache_action` enum ### 2. Stream Management in Observability Mode **v1.30:** - Streams closed immediately on filter destruction - Risk of message loss if processor still processing - No flow control support **v1.31:** - Streams deferred closure via thread-local manager - Processor gets `deferred_close_timeout` to finish - Flow control supported via watermark callbacks - Safer async handling ### 3. Error Response Status Code **v1.30:** - Timeout → **500 Internal Server Error** - May mislead about error source **v1.31:** - Timeout → **504 Gateway Timeout** - More accurate semantic for upstream timeouts - Clearer for monitoring/alerting ### 4. Tracing Context Propagation **v1.30:** - Tracing context headers not always propagated to ext_proc **v1.31:** - Tracing context headers consistently propagated in bidirectional streams - Better distributed tracing support - Improved observability --- ## Migration Steps ### Phase 1: Preparation (Pre-Upgrade) 1. **Audit Current Configuration:** ```bash grep -r "ext_proc\|external_processor" /path/to/configs/ | grep -E "yml|yaml" ``` 2. **Document Current Behavior:** - Note which ext_proc filters use `disable_clear_route_cache` - Record current route cache hit rates if monitored - Document timeout error handling code 3. **Test Timeout Error Handling:** - Simulate ext_proc service timeouts - Verify downstream services handle 500 errors - Plan for 504 response handling ### Phase 2: Configuration Update (Upgrade Compatible) 1. **Update Configurations to Use `route_cache_action`:** ```yaml # For each ext_proc filter ext_proc: # Remove: disable_clear_route_cache: true/false # Add one of: route_cache_action: DEFAULT # Recommended # OR route_cache_action: CLEAR # If always clearing # OR route_cache_action: RETAIN # If never clearing ``` 2. **If Using Observability Mode, Set Deferred Timeout:** ```yaml ext_proc: observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 5s # Adjust to your needs ``` 3. **Validate Configurations:** ```bash envoy -c /path/to/config.yaml --mode validate-only ``` ### Phase 3: Testing (Before Production Deployment) 1. **Unit/Integration Tests:** - Test ext_proc with various `route_cache_action` settings - Verify header mutation detection - Test timeout scenarios 2. **Performance Testing:** - Route cache hit rates with DEFAULT vs CLEAR - Stream cleanup timing with various `deferred_close_timeout` values - Memory usage in observability mode 3. **Error Handling Tests:** - Timeout responses (verify 504 status code) - Downstream client behavior with 504 vs 500 - Monitoring/alerting rule accuracy ### Phase 4: Staging Deployment 1. **Canary Deployment:** - Deploy v1.31 to small percentage of traffic - Monitor error rates and latency - Watch for 504 errors from ext_proc timeouts 2. **Configuration Validation:** - Verify all ext_proc filters use `route_cache_action` - Confirm deferred close timeout works correctly - Test observability mode if enabled 3. **Rollback Plan:** - Revert to v1.30 if critical issues found - Have configuration rollback prepared ### Phase 5: Production Deployment 1. **Gradual Rollout:** - Start with low traffic percentage - Gradually increase based on metrics - Monitor for issues 2. **Monitoring:** - Track `clear_route_cache_*` stats changes - Watch for 504 errors - Monitor route cache hit rates 3. **Post-Deployment Verification:** - Confirm expected route cache behavior - Validate error handling works - Review performance metrics ### Phase 6: Cleanup (Post-Deployment) 1. **Remove Old Comments:** - Update documentation to reflect v1.31 - Remove references to `disable_clear_route_cache` 2. **Optimize Configuration:** - Fine-tune `route_cache_action` based on metrics - Adjust `deferred_close_timeout` if needed 3. **Plan Future Deprecation:** - Mark calendar for removal of `disable_clear_route_cache` support - Plan removal of `async_mode` (being replaced by `observability_mode`) --- ## Testing Recommendations ### 1. Unit Tests **Route Cache Behavior:** ```python # Test DEFAULT mode with header mutation config.route_cache_action = DEFAULT config.disable_clear_route_cache = False response.clear_route_cache = True headers_mutated = True assert route_cache_cleared() == True # Test DEFAULT mode without header mutation config.route_cache_action = DEFAULT response.clear_route_cache = True headers_mutated = False assert route_cache_cleared() == False # Test CLEAR mode always clears config.route_cache_action = CLEAR response.clear_route_cache = False headers_mutated = True assert route_cache_cleared() == True # Test RETAIN mode never clears config.route_cache_action = RETAIN response.clear_route_cache = True headers_mutated = True assert route_cache_cleared() == False ``` **Status Code for Timeouts:** ```python # Simulate ext_proc timeout ext_proc_service.delay = 10 * timeout response = send_request() assert response.status_code == 504 # v1.31 change # (v1.30 would return 500) ``` ### 2. Integration Tests **Stream Closure in Observability Mode:** ```python config.observability_mode = True config.deferred_close_timeout = Duration(millis=1000) request = http_request() request.send() # Verify stream stays open during deferred_close_timeout assert stream_open() for 1 second # Verify stream closes after timeout await sleep(1.1 seconds) assert stream_closed() ``` **Multiple Ext_Proc Filters:** ```python # Test with different route_cache_action modes env.add_ext_proc(action=DEFAULT, name="default_filter") env.add_ext_proc(action=CLEAR, name="clear_filter") env.add_ext_proc(action=RETAIN, name="retain_filter") # Verify each behaves independently request.route_to("default_filter") assert default_behavior() request.route_to("clear_filter") assert clear_behavior() request.route_to("retain_filter") assert retain_behavior() ``` ### 3. Performance Tests **Route Cache Hit Rates:** ``` Metric: Route cache hit ratio Before: baseline After (DEFAULT): should be same or better After (CLEAR): likely lower After (RETAIN): likely higher (but may be incorrect) ``` **Stream Lifecycle:** ``` Metric: Memory usage per stream Observability mode with deferred closure: - Peak memory: ~same (stream still held) - Peak duration: longer (deferred wait) - Cleanup: cleaner (less premature closure) ``` **Error Handling Latency:** ``` Metric: Time to return 504 error Should be similar to previous 500 error time (Just different status code, same timeout) ``` ### 4. Load Testing **Concurrent Streams in Observability Mode:** ``` Config: - observability_mode: true - deferred_close_timeout: 5s Load: 1000 concurrent requests Measure: - Peak connection count (should be manageable) - Memory growth over time (should be bounded) - Stream closure rate (should be steady) ``` --- ## Rollback Guidance ### If Issues Occur After Upgrade **Immediate Rollback (0-5 minutes):** 1. Revert Envoy to v1.30.0 2. Keep old configuration (both formats supported) 3. Monitor metrics to verify behavior **Configuration Rollback (if needed):** ```yaml # If v1.31 config doesn't work # Revert to v1.30 style # Change this: ext_proc: route_cache_action: DEFAULT # Back to this: ext_proc: disable_clear_route_cache: false ``` ### Troubleshooting Common Issues **Issue: Configuration validation fails** ``` Error: "disable_clear_route_cache and route_cache_action can not be set to none-default at the same time." Solution: - Remove disable_clear_route_cache from config - Keep only route_cache_action ``` **Issue: Route cache not clearing as expected** ``` Problem: route_cache_action: DEFAULT but cache not cleared Diagnostic: 1. Check if headers are being mutated 2. Check if clear_route_cache flag is set by processor 3. Check upstream vs downstream filter chain Solution: - Use route_cache_action: CLEAR to force clearing - Or verify processor sets clear_route_cache: true ``` **Issue: Observability mode streams not closing** ``` Problem: gRPC connections to ext_proc staying open too long Solution: 1. Reduce deferred_close_timeout value 2. Verify processor is handling stream closure correctly 3. Check for processor-side hanging connections ``` **Issue: 504 errors being handled as 500** ``` Problem: Downstream expecting 500 from ext_proc timeout Solution: 1. Update error handling code to expect 504 2. Update monitoring/alerting rules 3. Verify load balancer doesn't treat 504 specially ``` --- ## Pro Tips for Successful Migration ### 1. Gradual Migration by Filter Migrate ext_proc filters one at a time rather than all at once: ```yaml # Filter 1: Already migrated (using route_cache_action) - name: ext_proc_v1 typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor route_cache_action: DEFAULT # Filter 2: Still using old style (still works) - name: ext_proc_v2 typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor disable_clear_route_cache: false ``` ### 2. Monitor Route Cache Statistics Track the stats to understand behavior: - `http.connection_manager.route_cache.hits` - `http.connection_manager.route_cache.misses` - `ext_proc.clear_route_cache_ignored` - `ext_proc.clear_route_cache_disabled` ### 3. Use DEFAULT Mode for Most Cases Unless you have specific requirements, use `route_cache_action: DEFAULT`: - Most compatible with v1.30 behavior - Respects processor decisions - Good performance ### 4. Test External Processor Timeout Handling Explicitly test what happens when ext_proc times out: ```bash # Simulate delay in ext_proc service # Observe that client receives 504 (not 500) # Verify retry logic handles 504 appropriately ``` ### 5. Document Configuration Choices For each ext_proc filter, document why you chose a particular `route_cache_action`: ```yaml ext_proc: # Using CLEAR because this processor frequently modifies routing headers # and we need routes to be recalculated every time route_cache_action: CLEAR grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster ``` --- ## Deprecated Features Timeline | Feature | Deprecated | Removal Target | Status | |---------|-----------|-----------------|--------| | `disable_clear_route_cache` | v1.31 | v1.32+ | Still functional, use `route_cache_action` | | `async_mode` | v1.30+ | TBD | Hidden from API; use `observability_mode` | --- ## Reference ### Proto Messages **New Enum (v1.31):** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { enum RouteCacheAction { // DEFAULT: Clear cache only if processor sets clear_route_cache AND headers mutated DEFAULT = 0; // CLEAR: Always clear cache (ignore processor's clear_route_cache flag) CLEAR = 1; // RETAIN: Never clear cache (ignore processor's clear_route_cache flag) RETAIN = 2; } } ``` **New Field (v1.31):** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { // Specifies the action to be taken when an external processor response // is received in response to request headers. RouteCacheAction route_cache_action = 18; // Timeout for deferred stream closure in observability mode. // Default: 5000 milliseconds google.protobuf.Duration deferred_close_timeout = 19; } ``` **Deprecated Field (still present in v1.31):** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { // DEPRECATED: Use route_cache_action instead bool disable_clear_route_cache = 11; } ``` ### Related Files **Proto Definitions:** - `api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/ext_proc.proto` **Implementation:** - `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/ext_proc.cc` - `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/ext_proc.h` - `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/processor_state.cc` - `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/client_impl.cc` **Documentation:** - `docs/root/configuration/http/http_filters/ext_proc_filter.rst` ### Key Statistics **New stats (v1.31):** None new **Updated stats (v1.31):** - `clear_route_cache_ignored`: Now incremented when no header mutation detected - `clear_route_cache_disabled`: Now incremented when `route_cache_action: RETAIN` --- ## Support and Questions For additional help with the migration: 1. Review the [Envoy documentation](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/http/http_filters/ext_proc_filter) 2. Check the [release notes](https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/releases) for v1.31.0 3. Consult the [changelog](https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/main/changelogs/current.yaml) 4. Engage with the [Envoy community](https://www.envoyproxy.io/community) --- **Document Version:** 1.0 **Envoy Versions Covered:** v1.30.0 → v1.31.0 **Last Updated:** March 1, 2026

- DEFAULT: Cache cleared
- CLEAR: Cache cleared
- RETAIN: NOT cleared | | Upstream filter chain | Route cache NOT cleared | Route cache NOT cleared (upstream route cache not clearable) | **Configuration Examples:** Before (v1.30): ```yaml ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster disable_clear_route_cache: false # Default behavior ``` After (v1.31) - Equivalent configurations: ```yaml # Option 1: DEFAULT behavior (matches v1.30 behavior) ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: DEFAULT # Option 2: Always clear (when mutations occur) ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: CLEAR # Option 3: Never clear (equivalent to v1.30's disable_clear_route_cache=true) ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: RETAIN ``` **Backward Compatibility Note:** The field `disable_clear_route_cache` is still supported in v1.31 with a migration annotation. When `disable_clear_route_cache=true` is set, it is automatically converted to `route_cache_action=RETAIN`. However, **you cannot set both fields to non-default values** - this will cause a configuration validation error: > "disable_clear_route_cache and route_cache_action can not be set to none-default at the same time." --- ### 2. External Processor Timeout Error Status Code **What Changed:** Timeout errors from the external processor now return HTTP 504 (Gateway Timeout) instead of HTTP 500 (Internal Server Error). **Impact Level:** LOW **Details:** **v1.30 Behavior:** When a message timeout occurred waiting for a response from the external processor: - Return status code: **500 Internal Server Error** - Error response was sent to downstream clients **v1.31 Behavior:** Same timeout condition: - Return status code: **504 Gateway Timeout** - More semantically correct for upstream service timeouts **Why This Matters:** - Downstream clients may differentiate handling based on status code - Monitoring/alerting systems may track 5xx vs 4xx errors differently - Load balancers may handle 504 differently than 500 (some don't retry 504s by default) **Migration Action:** - Review downstream client error handling code - Update monitoring/alerting rules that count 500 errors - Verify load balancer retry policies handle 504 appropriately - Test retry behavior with external processor timeouts --- ## Deprecated Features ### 1. `disable_clear_route_cache` Field (DEPRECATION) **Deprecation Status:** DEPRECATED (still functional in v1.31, planned for removal in v1.32+) **Details:** The boolean field `disable_clear_route_cache` is now superseded by the `route_cache_action` enum: ```protobuf // v1.30 & v1.31 (deprecated in v1.31) bool disable_clear_route_cache = 11; // v1.31 (new, recommended) RouteCacheAction route_cache_action = 18; ``` **Migration Path:** | Current Configuration | Migration Path | |----------------------|----------------| | `disable_clear_route_cache: false` (default) | Use `route_cache_action: DEFAULT` or omit (DEFAULT is the default) | | `disable_clear_route_cache: true` | Use `route_cache_action: RETAIN` | | Not set (defaults to false) | No action needed; system defaults to DEFAULT behavior | **Recommended Timeline:** 1. **Immediately:** Migrate to `route_cache_action` in new configurations 2. **Within 1-2 releases:** Update existing configurations to use `route_cache_action` 3. **Future release:** `disable_clear_route_cache` will be removed --- ## New Features ### 1. Route Cache Action Enum **Availability:** v1.31.0+ **Purpose:** Provides three distinct strategies for handling route cache clearing in response to external processor responses. **Configuration Field:** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { enum RouteCacheAction { DEFAULT = 0; CLEAR = 1; RETAIN = 2; } RouteCacheAction route_cache_action = 18; } ``` **Use Cases:** - **DEFAULT (recommended):** Respects the external processor's `clear_route_cache` flag only when header mutations are present. Best for most use cases where the processor decides when cache clearing is needed. - **CLEAR:** Always clears the route cache when the external processor responds to request headers. Useful when you want to ensure routes are recalculated after every ext_proc interaction, regardless of the processor's response. - **RETAIN:** Never clears the route cache, ignoring any `clear_route_cache` requests from the processor. Use when you're confident routes don't change or want to maximize route cache hit rates. **Configuration Examples:** ```yaml # Example 1: DEFAULT mode (recommended) - name: ext_proc_default typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: DEFAULT processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND response_header_mode: SEND # Example 2: CLEAR mode (always refresh routes) - name: ext_proc_always_clear typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: CLEAR processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND response_header_mode: SEND # Example 3: RETAIN mode (never clear cache) - name: ext_proc_never_clear typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster route_cache_action: RETAIN processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND response_header_mode: SEND ``` --- ### 2. Deferred Stream Closure Timeout **Availability:** v1.31.0+ **Purpose:** Controls how long gRPC streams to the external processor remain open after filter destruction in observability mode. **Configuration Field:** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { google.protobuf.Duration deferred_close_timeout = 19; } ``` **Default Value:** 5000 milliseconds (5 seconds) **Details:** In observability mode (`observability_mode: true`), the external processor stream may be kept open independently of the main HTTP request/response stream. This timeout controls how long Envoy will wait before forcefully closing that stream if it hasn't closed gracefully. **When This Matters:** - **Observability mode enabled:** The external processor receives messages asynchronously and may process them after the HTTP response completes - **Filter destruction:** When the filter is destroyed (request complete), instead of immediately closing the stream, it defers the closure - **Timeout:** If the stream doesn't close naturally within `deferred_close_timeout`, Envoy forcefully terminates it **Configuration Examples:** ```yaml # Example 1: Default timeout (5 seconds) ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 5s # Optional, this is the default # Example 2: Shorter timeout (1 second) for faster cleanup ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 1s # Example 3: Longer timeout (10 seconds) to allow more processing time ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 10s ``` **Performance Implications:** - **Too short:** External processor may not complete processing; stream closed prematurely - **Too long:** More memory/connection overhead; slower resource cleanup - **Optimal:** Choose based on typical external processor response time **Related to Observability Mode:** The `deferred_close_timeout` is specifically for the async/deferred handling in observability mode: ```yaml ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true # Enable "Send and Go" mode deferred_close_timeout: 5s # Control cleanup timing processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND request_body_mode: STREAMED response_header_mode: SEND response_body_mode: STREAMED ``` In observability mode: 1. Envoy sends messages to the external processor without waiting for responses 2. The processor observes data but responses are ignored 3. When the HTTP stream ends, the ext_proc stream is marked for deferred closure 4. After `deferred_close_timeout` expires, the stream is forcefully closed --- ### 3. Enhanced Observability Mode **Availability:** v1.31.0+ (improved from v1.30) **Enhancements:** 1. **Better Stream Lifecycle Management:** Streams are now managed by a thread-local stream manager, preventing premature closure while the external processor is still processing 2. **Deferred Closure:** Streams remain open for a configured duration after the HTTP request completes, allowing the external processor to complete its work 3. **Flow Control Support:** Added support for gRPC side-stream flow control with watermark callbacks 4. **Safer Async Processing:** Improved handling of filter destruction timing to prevent use-after-free bugs **Configuration:** ```yaml ext_proc: grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 5s processing_mode: request_header_mode: SEND request_body_mode: STREAMED response_header_mode: SEND response_body_mode: STREAMED ``` **Key Points:** - The external processor should **not** send response messages; they are ignored - Only STREAMED and NONE body processing modes are supported - Flow control is now available to prevent overwhelming the processor - Deferred closure ensures graceful stream cleanup --- ## Configuration Updates ### Step 1: Identify Current Usage of `disable_clear_route_cache` Search your Envoy configurations for any use of `disable_clear_route_cache`: ```bash # Find all ext_proc configurations with disable_clear_route_cache grep -r "disable_clear_route_cache" /path/to/configs/ ``` ### Step 2: Update Route Cache Configuration For each configuration using `disable_clear_route_cache`: **If `disable_clear_route_cache: false` or omitted (default):** ```yaml # Old ext_proc: disable_clear_route_cache: false # New (explicit, recommended) ext_proc: route_cache_action: DEFAULT ``` **If `disable_clear_route_cache: true`:** ```yaml # Old ext_proc: disable_clear_route_cache: true # New ext_proc: route_cache_action: RETAIN ``` ### Step 3: Consider Using CLEAR Mode if Applicable If you want to ensure routes are always refreshed after ext_proc processing: ```yaml ext_proc: route_cache_action: CLEAR grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster ``` ### Step 4: Configure Deferred Close Timeout (if using observability_mode) If observability mode is enabled, explicitly set the deferred close timeout based on your workload: ```yaml ext_proc: observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 5s # Adjust based on processor response time grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster ``` ### Step 5: Test Error Handling for 504 Responses Update any downstream error handling code that specifically catches or handles 500 errors from ext_proc timeouts: ```python # Example: If you had code like this try: response = requests.get(envoy_url) except requests.HTTPError as e: if e.response.status_code == 500: # Might have been an ext_proc timeout # Now it will be 504 instead except requests.HTTPError as e: if e.response.status_code == 504: # This is now the ext_proc timeout code ``` --- ## Behavioral Changes ### 1. Route Cache Clearing Logic **v1.30 Logic:** ``` if clear_route_cache AND NOT disable_clear_route_cache: clear_route_cache() else: do_nothing() ``` **v1.31 Logic:** ``` if no header mutation: do_nothing() # No reason to clear else: switch route_cache_action: case DEFAULT: if clear_route_cache: clear_route_cache() case CLEAR: clear_route_cache() # Always case RETAIN: do_nothing() # Never ``` **Key Difference:** v1.31 adds a check for header mutations, preventing unnecessary cache clearing when headers aren't modified. **Impact:** - Fewer unnecessary route cache clears - Better performance in scenarios with many header-only requests - More explicit control through `route_cache_action` enum ### 2. Stream Management in Observability Mode **v1.30:** - Streams closed immediately on filter destruction - Risk of message loss if processor still processing - No flow control support **v1.31:** - Streams deferred closure via thread-local manager - Processor gets `deferred_close_timeout` to finish - Flow control supported via watermark callbacks - Safer async handling ### 3. Error Response Status Code **v1.30:** - Timeout → **500 Internal Server Error** - May mislead about error source **v1.31:** - Timeout → **504 Gateway Timeout** - More accurate semantic for upstream timeouts - Clearer for monitoring/alerting ### 4. Tracing Context Propagation **v1.30:** - Tracing context headers not always propagated to ext_proc **v1.31:** - Tracing context headers consistently propagated in bidirectional streams - Better distributed tracing support - Improved observability --- ## Migration Steps ### Phase 1: Preparation (Pre-Upgrade) 1. **Audit Current Configuration:** ```bash grep -r "ext_proc\|external_processor" /path/to/configs/ | grep -E "yml|yaml" ``` 2. **Document Current Behavior:** - Note which ext_proc filters use `disable_clear_route_cache` - Record current route cache hit rates if monitored - Document timeout error handling code 3. **Test Timeout Error Handling:** - Simulate ext_proc service timeouts - Verify downstream services handle 500 errors - Plan for 504 response handling ### Phase 2: Configuration Update (Upgrade Compatible) 1. **Update Configurations to Use `route_cache_action`:** ```yaml # For each ext_proc filter ext_proc: # Remove: disable_clear_route_cache: true/false # Add one of: route_cache_action: DEFAULT # Recommended # OR route_cache_action: CLEAR # If always clearing # OR route_cache_action: RETAIN # If never clearing ``` 2. **If Using Observability Mode, Set Deferred Timeout:** ```yaml ext_proc: observability_mode: true deferred_close_timeout: 5s # Adjust to your needs ``` 3. **Validate Configurations:** ```bash envoy -c /path/to/config.yaml --mode validate-only ``` ### Phase 3: Testing (Before Production Deployment) 1. **Unit/Integration Tests:** - Test ext_proc with various `route_cache_action` settings - Verify header mutation detection - Test timeout scenarios 2. **Performance Testing:** - Route cache hit rates with DEFAULT vs CLEAR - Stream cleanup timing with various `deferred_close_timeout` values - Memory usage in observability mode 3. **Error Handling Tests:** - Timeout responses (verify 504 status code) - Downstream client behavior with 504 vs 500 - Monitoring/alerting rule accuracy ### Phase 4: Staging Deployment 1. **Canary Deployment:** - Deploy v1.31 to small percentage of traffic - Monitor error rates and latency - Watch for 504 errors from ext_proc timeouts 2. **Configuration Validation:** - Verify all ext_proc filters use `route_cache_action` - Confirm deferred close timeout works correctly - Test observability mode if enabled 3. **Rollback Plan:** - Revert to v1.30 if critical issues found - Have configuration rollback prepared ### Phase 5: Production Deployment 1. **Gradual Rollout:** - Start with low traffic percentage - Gradually increase based on metrics - Monitor for issues 2. **Monitoring:** - Track `clear_route_cache_*` stats changes - Watch for 504 errors - Monitor route cache hit rates 3. **Post-Deployment Verification:** - Confirm expected route cache behavior - Validate error handling works - Review performance metrics ### Phase 6: Cleanup (Post-Deployment) 1. **Remove Old Comments:** - Update documentation to reflect v1.31 - Remove references to `disable_clear_route_cache` 2. **Optimize Configuration:** - Fine-tune `route_cache_action` based on metrics - Adjust `deferred_close_timeout` if needed 3. **Plan Future Deprecation:** - Mark calendar for removal of `disable_clear_route_cache` support - Plan removal of `async_mode` (being replaced by `observability_mode`) --- ## Testing Recommendations ### 1. Unit Tests **Route Cache Behavior:** ```python # Test DEFAULT mode with header mutation config.route_cache_action = DEFAULT config.disable_clear_route_cache = False response.clear_route_cache = True headers_mutated = True assert route_cache_cleared() == True # Test DEFAULT mode without header mutation config.route_cache_action = DEFAULT response.clear_route_cache = True headers_mutated = False assert route_cache_cleared() == False # Test CLEAR mode always clears config.route_cache_action = CLEAR response.clear_route_cache = False headers_mutated = True assert route_cache_cleared() == True # Test RETAIN mode never clears config.route_cache_action = RETAIN response.clear_route_cache = True headers_mutated = True assert route_cache_cleared() == False ``` **Status Code for Timeouts:** ```python # Simulate ext_proc timeout ext_proc_service.delay = 10 * timeout response = send_request() assert response.status_code == 504 # v1.31 change # (v1.30 would return 500) ``` ### 2. Integration Tests **Stream Closure in Observability Mode:** ```python config.observability_mode = True config.deferred_close_timeout = Duration(millis=1000) request = http_request() request.send() # Verify stream stays open during deferred_close_timeout assert stream_open() for 1 second # Verify stream closes after timeout await sleep(1.1 seconds) assert stream_closed() ``` **Multiple Ext_Proc Filters:** ```python # Test with different route_cache_action modes env.add_ext_proc(action=DEFAULT, name="default_filter") env.add_ext_proc(action=CLEAR, name="clear_filter") env.add_ext_proc(action=RETAIN, name="retain_filter") # Verify each behaves independently request.route_to("default_filter") assert default_behavior() request.route_to("clear_filter") assert clear_behavior() request.route_to("retain_filter") assert retain_behavior() ``` ### 3. Performance Tests **Route Cache Hit Rates:** ``` Metric: Route cache hit ratio Before: baseline After (DEFAULT): should be same or better After (CLEAR): likely lower After (RETAIN): likely higher (but may be incorrect) ``` **Stream Lifecycle:** ``` Metric: Memory usage per stream Observability mode with deferred closure: - Peak memory: ~same (stream still held) - Peak duration: longer (deferred wait) - Cleanup: cleaner (less premature closure) ``` **Error Handling Latency:** ``` Metric: Time to return 504 error Should be similar to previous 500 error time (Just different status code, same timeout) ``` ### 4. Load Testing **Concurrent Streams in Observability Mode:** ``` Config: - observability_mode: true - deferred_close_timeout: 5s Load: 1000 concurrent requests Measure: - Peak connection count (should be manageable) - Memory growth over time (should be bounded) - Stream closure rate (should be steady) ``` --- ## Rollback Guidance ### If Issues Occur After Upgrade **Immediate Rollback (0-5 minutes):** 1. Revert Envoy to v1.30.0 2. Keep old configuration (both formats supported) 3. Monitor metrics to verify behavior **Configuration Rollback (if needed):** ```yaml # If v1.31 config doesn't work # Revert to v1.30 style # Change this: ext_proc: route_cache_action: DEFAULT # Back to this: ext_proc: disable_clear_route_cache: false ``` ### Troubleshooting Common Issues **Issue: Configuration validation fails** ``` Error: "disable_clear_route_cache and route_cache_action can not be set to none-default at the same time." Solution: - Remove disable_clear_route_cache from config - Keep only route_cache_action ``` **Issue: Route cache not clearing as expected** ``` Problem: route_cache_action: DEFAULT but cache not cleared Diagnostic: 1. Check if headers are being mutated 2. Check if clear_route_cache flag is set by processor 3. Check upstream vs downstream filter chain Solution: - Use route_cache_action: CLEAR to force clearing - Or verify processor sets clear_route_cache: true ``` **Issue: Observability mode streams not closing** ``` Problem: gRPC connections to ext_proc staying open too long Solution: 1. Reduce deferred_close_timeout value 2. Verify processor is handling stream closure correctly 3. Check for processor-side hanging connections ``` **Issue: 504 errors being handled as 500** ``` Problem: Downstream expecting 500 from ext_proc timeout Solution: 1. Update error handling code to expect 504 2. Update monitoring/alerting rules 3. Verify load balancer doesn't treat 504 specially ``` --- ## Pro Tips for Successful Migration ### 1. Gradual Migration by Filter Migrate ext_proc filters one at a time rather than all at once: ```yaml # Filter 1: Already migrated (using route_cache_action) - name: ext_proc_v1 typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor route_cache_action: DEFAULT # Filter 2: Still using old style (still works) - name: ext_proc_v2 typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor disable_clear_route_cache: false ``` ### 2. Monitor Route Cache Statistics Track the stats to understand behavior: - `http.connection_manager.route_cache.hits` - `http.connection_manager.route_cache.misses` - `ext_proc.clear_route_cache_ignored` - `ext_proc.clear_route_cache_disabled` ### 3. Use DEFAULT Mode for Most Cases Unless you have specific requirements, use `route_cache_action: DEFAULT`: - Most compatible with v1.30 behavior - Respects processor decisions - Good performance ### 4. Test External Processor Timeout Handling Explicitly test what happens when ext_proc times out: ```bash # Simulate delay in ext_proc service # Observe that client receives 504 (not 500) # Verify retry logic handles 504 appropriately ``` ### 5. Document Configuration Choices For each ext_proc filter, document why you chose a particular `route_cache_action`: ```yaml ext_proc: # Using CLEAR because this processor frequently modifies routing headers # and we need routes to be recalculated every time route_cache_action: CLEAR grpc_service: envoy_grpc: cluster_name: ext_proc_cluster ``` --- ## Deprecated Features Timeline | Feature | Deprecated | Removal Target | Status | |---------|-----------|-----------------|--------| | `disable_clear_route_cache` | v1.31 | v1.32+ | Still functional, use `route_cache_action` | | `async_mode` | v1.30+ | TBD | Hidden from API; use `observability_mode` | --- ## Reference ### Proto Messages **New Enum (v1.31):** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { enum RouteCacheAction { // DEFAULT: Clear cache only if processor sets clear_route_cache AND headers mutated DEFAULT = 0; // CLEAR: Always clear cache (ignore processor's clear_route_cache flag) CLEAR = 1; // RETAIN: Never clear cache (ignore processor's clear_route_cache flag) RETAIN = 2; } } ``` **New Field (v1.31):** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { // Specifies the action to be taken when an external processor response // is received in response to request headers. RouteCacheAction route_cache_action = 18; // Timeout for deferred stream closure in observability mode. // Default: 5000 milliseconds google.protobuf.Duration deferred_close_timeout = 19; } ``` **Deprecated Field (still present in v1.31):** ```protobuf message ExternalProcessor { // DEPRECATED: Use route_cache_action instead bool disable_clear_route_cache = 11; } ``` ### Related Files **Proto Definitions:** - `api/envoy/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/v3/ext_proc.proto` **Implementation:** - `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/ext_proc.cc` - `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/ext_proc.h` - `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/processor_state.cc` - `source/extensions/filters/http/ext_proc/client_impl.cc` **Documentation:** - `docs/root/configuration/http/http_filters/ext_proc_filter.rst` ### Key Statistics **New stats (v1.31):** None new **Updated stats (v1.31):** - `clear_route_cache_ignored`: Now incremented when no header mutation detected - `clear_route_cache_disabled`: Now incremented when `route_cache_action: RETAIN` --- ## Support and Questions For additional help with the migration: 1. Review the [Envoy documentation](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/http/http_filters/ext_proc_filter) 2. Check the [release notes](https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/releases) for v1.31.0 3. Consult the [changelog](https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/main/changelogs/current.yaml) 4. Engage with the [Envoy community](https://www.envoyproxy.io/community) --- **Document Version:** 1.0 **Envoy Versions Covered:** v1.30.0 → v1.31.0 **Last Updated:** March 1, 2026 ", "structuredPatch": [], "originalFile": null}
